privacy.p_scope: This policy describes how the public marketing website at meshchatx.com (the "Site") processes information when you visit pages on the Site. It does not govern the MeshChatX application, Reticulum, or third-party services you may use separately.
privacy.p_analytics: This public Site uses Swetrix, a privacy-friendly analytics tool, to understand which platforms need better support and to gauge interest in MeshChatX. Swetrix is GDPR-compliant, does not use cookies, and does not track you across the web.
privacy.p_analytics_detail: The home and download areas read version information from a static file we serve from the same site. Page-view data is sent to our self-hosted Swetrix instance (swtrx.meshchatx.com). This helps us decide which platforms to prioritise and understand overall interest in the project. No personal data is collected, no cookies are set, and your browsing activity is not shared with third-party analytics services. If you use download or source links, those requests go to the host you click through to.
privacy.p_rights: Depending on where you live, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, or restrict processing of personal data, and to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. This marketing Site is designed to keep processing minimal. If you have a specific request regarding this Site, contact us as below and we will respond as required by applicable law.
privacy.p_contact: For privacy questions about this Site, see the contact page.

hero.lead: MeshChatX is a all-in-one Reticulum client featuring messaging, calling, and a nomadnet browser.

section.infra_lead: Built for mesh networks and censorship resistance.

feature.decentralized_p: Built on Reticulum. No central servers, no single point of failure. Total ownership of your data.
feature.crypto_p: Reticulum uses efficient, strong, and well-tested cryptographic primitives designed for safety and reliability across all platforms.
feature.mesh_p: Communicate across any medium. MeshChatX works over the internet, radio, LoRa, and many other mediums!
feature.platform_p: Officially supported on Desktop (Electron) and Web (Docker/Python). Android is available via Termux.

security.lead: Verification, transparency, and conservative defaults in the desktop stack, so you know what shipped and how it behaves.
security.bullet_slsa: SLSA Level 3 with hardened supply-chain provenance for release builds.
security.bullet_asar: ASAR integrity monitoring helps detect tampering with packaged application resources.
security.bullet_landlock: Landlock LSM sandboxing on Linux to restrict filesystem access.
security.bullet_sigstore: Sigstore cosign-based signing for verifiable artifacts where applicable.
security.bullet_sast: Static analysis and dependency scanning with Trivy, pip-audit, Ruff, and CodeQL in the delivery pipeline.
security.bullet_csp: CSP plus tight defaults for remote content in the Electron shell (including CORS discipline where it applies).
security.bullet_transport: HTTPS and WSS by default for remote pages and transports where supported.

split.item1_p: Create and manage as many identities as you need. Switch between personas seamlessly while keeping your data private and compartmentalized.
split.item2_p: No government ID, phone number, or email required. Access the network anonymously without any third-party verification or hurdles.
split.item3_p: Built for mesh networks and censorship resistance. Your messages propagate automatically and local archiving ensures your data is always available.

roadmap.lead: The MeshChatX development roadmap. Here is what we are working on and what is coming next.
roadmap.notice: Roadmap is subject to change. Versions and features may be adjusted based on development priorities and community feedback.
